text: The surgeon dissects the root of the left hepatic vein and encircles it with umbilical tape, creating a sling that extends along the caudate groove to the gallbladder fossa. This sling visually marks the planned transection plane, which follows the middle hepatic vein and divides a segment IV branch and the main left hepatic vein.

The surgeon divides the left hepatic artery and the segment IV hepatic artery at the porta hepatis. The left portal vein and the left hepatic duct are also divided, indicating progression in the resection of the left liver lobe.
